events.title: First public commit by Jia Tan
events.summary: PR to libarchive that subtly weakens input sanitization (replaces safe_fprintf with fprintf). Merged.

events.title: First pressure email to xz-devel
events.summary: Sock-puppet email complaining that Jia Tan's patch isn't being merged.
events.quote: Patches spend years on this mailing list. There is no reason to think anything is coming soon.

events.title: Lasse mentions Jia Tan as future co-maintainer
events.summary: Reply on xz-devel hinting Jia Tan might have a bigger role in the future.
events.quote: Jia Tan has helped me off-list with XZ Utils and he might have a bigger role in the future at least with XZ Utils.

events.title: Lasse pushes back on the pressure
events.summary: Discloses ongoing burnout and longterm mental-health struggles, hinting Jia Tan will likely take a bigger role.
events.quote: I haven't lost interest but my ability to care has been fairly limited mostly due to longterm mental health issues but also due to some other things.

events.title: Second pressure email
events.summary: Demands new maintainer; suggests delays are unacceptable.
events.quote: With your current rate, I very doubt to see 5.4.0 release this year. The only progress since april has been small changes to test code.

events.title: Jia Tan listed as co-maintainer
events.summary: README.md updated to list both Lasse and Jia Tan as project maintainers.

events.title: Jia Tan takes over releases
events.summary: v5.4.1 ships — the final release Lasse signs. Subsequent releases are signed by Jia Tan.

events.title: PR to disable IFUNC fuzzing in oss-fuzz
events.summary: Jia Tan asks Google's oss-fuzz to disable IFUNC checks — a detail that only reads as suspicious in retrospect.

events.title: Removes Landlock sandbox feature-test from CMakeLists
events.summary: Small but later-noted hostile preparation; reduces sandboxing on the build.

events.title: Malicious test files committed
events.summary: Adds tests/files/bad-3-corrupt_lzma2.xz and good-large_compressed.lzma — disguised as test corpus, actually contain the obfuscated payload.

events.title: xz-utils 5.6.0 released
events.summary: Tarball ships with a modified m4/build-to-host.m4 that triggers the payload during ./configure. The modification exists only in the tarball, not in git.

events.title: xz-utils 5.6.1 released
events.summary: Updated payload — fixes Valgrind errors that were tipping off Debian's autopkgtest. The hurried fix is itself a tell.

events.title: An anomaly during PostgreSQL benchmarking
events.summary: While micro-benchmarking PostgreSQL on Debian sid, Andres Freund notices that ssh logins to his test box are taking ~500 ms longer than they should. He files it under "weird" and keeps benchmarking.

events.title: Valgrind trace points at liblzma
events.summary: He runs valgrind on sshd, sees uninitialized-memory errors firing inside liblzma's CRC64 path. That is the wrong dependency — sshd doesn't link liblzma. The Debian sd_notify patch is the bridge that brings it in.

trace.comment: sshd → libsystemd → liblzma · the Debian sd_notify patch is the bridge

events.title: Public disclosure on oss-security
events.summary: He posts to oss-security at 08:51 UTC. Subject: "backdoor in upstream xz/liblzma leading to ssh server compromise."
events.quote: After observing a few odd symptoms around liblzma (part of the xz package) on Debian sid installations over the last weeks (logins with ssh taking a lot of CPU, valgrind errors) I figured out the answer: The upstream xz repository and the xz tarballs have been backdoored.

events.title: Distros roll back, GitHub disables the org
events.summary: Within hours: Debian rolls sid back to 5.4.5-0.2; Red Hat issues an urgent advisory pulling Fedora 40 Beta and Rawhide; Homebrew downgrades xz on macOS. GitHub disables the tukaani-project organization. CVE-2024-3094 is assigned the same day, scored 10.0.
